skill.name: read-github
skill.description:
Read and search GitHub repository documentation via gitmcp.io MCP service.

**WHEN TO USE:**
- User provides a GitHub URL
- User mentions a specific repo in owner/repo format
- User asks "what does this repo do?", "read the docs for X repo", or similar
- User wants to search code or docs within a repo
skill.summary: Fetch and search GitHub repository documentation using gitmcp.io MCP service
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate documentation access skill. The external commands (subprocess.Popen) spawn npx to connect to gitmcp.io MCP servers for fetching repository documentation. Network connections are limited to the gitmcp.io service URL. The static findings flagged regex patterns as 'weak cryptographic algorithms' which is a false positive - these are URL parsing regex, not crypto code. No command injection risk as arguments are passed properly. No prompt injection or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: subprocess.Popen spawns npx to connect to MCP servers. This is intentional design for the skill - it connects to gitmcp.io to fetch documentation. Arguments are passed as list, not shell=True, preventing command injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.Popen with hardcoded 'npx' command and URL argument is the expected MCP protocol behavior. No user input directly concatenated into shell commands.

low_findings.title: Network Connections to External Service
low_findings.description: The skill connects to gitmcp.io to fetch repository documentation. This is the core functionality of the skill - accessing GitHub repo docs via the MCP service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: gitmcp.io is a legitimate documentation service. Network connections are limited to this specific service URL.

content.value_statement: This skill enables AI agents to fetch and search GitHub repository documentation. It solves the problem of understanding unfamiliar codebases by connecting to the gitmcp.io MCP service.
actual_capabilities: Fetch complete documentation from any public GitHub repository
Search within repository documentation using semantic search
Search code within repositories using exact match
List available MCP tools for a given repository
Fetch external URLs referenced in documentation
limitations: Semantic search quality depends on MCP server implementation
Requires npx to be installed for MCP remote functionality

use_cases.title: Developer Learning New Libraries
use_cases.description: A developer wants to understand how to use an unfamiliar library. They provide the GitHub repo and the skill fetches the documentation to answer their questions.

use_cases.title: AI Agent Researching Codebases
use_cases.description: An AI agent needs to understand a specific codebase to help with a task. It uses the skill to fetch docs and search for relevant code patterns.

use_cases.title: Documentation Verification
use_cases.description: A technical writer wants to verify how their library documentation appears when fetched via MCP tools.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Documentation Fetch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the read-github skill to fetch the documentation for the repository owner/repo. Tell me what this project does.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to understand a repository

prompt_templates.title: Feature Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the read-github skill to search the documentation of owner/repo for information about [specific feature]. How do I use this feature?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to learn about a specific feature

prompt_templates.title: Code Implementation Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the read-github skill to search the code in owner/repo for the function or class named [function_name]. Show me how it is implemented.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to see implementation details

prompt_templates.title: External Reference Fetch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the read-github skill to find URLs mentioned in the owner/repo documentation, then fetch content from [specific URL].
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to follow external links from docs

output_examples.input: Use read-github to fetch docs for karpathy/llm-council
output_examples.output: The llm-council project is a library for running multiple LLM instances in parallel...

output_examples.input: Search the docs of facebook/react for 'useEffect hook'
output_examples.output: useEffect is a React hook that performs side effects in function components...

output_examples.input: Find the implementation of 'main' function in owner/repo
output_examples.output: The main function is defined in src/index.js at line 42...

best_practices: Fetch documentation first before attempting searches
Use full owner/repo format for clarity
Iterate on search queries for better results
anti_patterns: Assuming the skill can modify repositories (it only reads)
Using vague queries that return unrelated results

faq.question: Does this skill work with private repositories?
faq.answer: No, this skill only accesses public repositories through gitmcp.io.

faq.question: What is gitmcp.io?
faq.answer: gitmcp.io is a service that provides MCP (Model Context Protocol) access to GitHub repository documentation.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: You need npx available in your PATH to run the MCP remote commands.

faq.question: Can this skill write to repositories?
faq.answer: No, this skill is read-only. It only fetches documentation and searches code.

faq.question: What if a repository has no documentation?
faq.answer: The skill will return an empty or minimal response if no documentation is found.

faq.question: How does this work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: The skill provides instructions for Claude Code to use the gitmcp.py script or call MCP tools directly.
